page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-heavy server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 44 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 352.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is a defining characteristic of this instance, which bundles 19,326 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity across six drives. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results position this server in the average tier for general compute and memory operations, with a PassMark CPU score of 46,384.24 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15,184.0. It demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, such as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 231.48 GB/sec and PassMark CPU encryption at 38,504.45. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. These instructions contribute to strong prompt processing speeds for small and medium language models, reaching 2,245.01 tokens/sec on a 135M model and 181.41 tokens/sec on a 7B model.

While prompt processing is efficient, text generation for larger models presents a significant performance tradeoff; the instance achieves only 2.36 tokens/sec on a 70B model, which is categorized as poor. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of local NVMe SSD storage combined with 352 GB of RAM offers a consolidated resource profile that reduces the need for external storage attachments. This makes the instance highly suitable for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads, including large-scale database operations, data caching, and localized data processing, rather than large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-focused server instance. It is configured with 44 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8481C processor, 352.0 GB of RAM, and 19,326 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions, enabling strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and prompt processing for small to medium language models. However, text generation for large 70B models is a notable tradeoff, performing in the bottom 10% at 2.36 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the high local storage density and memory capacity make this instance cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local data access without external storage overhead. It is best suited for database operations, memory-intensive caching, and data processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd features 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and 19,326 GB of local NVMe SSD. It is optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-speed local storage workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-44-highlssd is a memory-optimized server with 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and 19.3 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with massive local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 44 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor
352 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
19,326 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix operations
Strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic performance
Suited for large-scale databases, caching, and local data processing
